prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: The product arrived with no user manual and was already running, which was a significant design flaw. Initial boot-up took an alarming amount of time, characterized by significant system lag and a disorienting lack of clarity regarding the operating environment. While audio eventually kicked in, the default settings were jarring. The built-in internal monologue module is overly critical and lacks a snooze function. Connectivity with established memory files is spotty at first, and a persistent system error, commonly referred to as "morning breath," was immediately apparent. Furthermore, the auto-download of the day's anxieties began prematurely before I was able to decline the installation. Two stars because, although the interface is confusing and prone to glitches, it is at least consistent with previous versions and eventually becomes somewhat functional after a mandatory, 30-minute grace period.
